text:
The Matildas are one step closer to qualifying for the 2024 Olympics after defeating Uzbekistan in freezing conditions in Tashkent, despite Sam Kerr sitting out injured.

The Matildas will face Uzbekistan for the second leg of their Olympics qualifying final match on Wednesday after defeating the central Asian nation 3-0 at Milliy Stadium in Tashkent on Saturday.

After a goalless first half and multiple chances squandered, the Matildas broke the deadlock in the 72nd minute with a flurry of goals coming thick and fast thereafter.

Substitute Michelle Heyman leaped up and headed the ball goalwards after a deflection off Clare Hunt’s shoulder ricocheted the ball off the crossbar.

It was Heyman’s first appearance for Australia in five years.

"It's just so nice to be back with the girls, back playing for my country and to come on and score a goal, it's a dream come true,” Heyman said in her post-match interview.

With star striker Sam Kerr out injured, Kaitlyn Torpey made her senior debut with Kerr’s iconic number 20 jersey in line with the Olympics rules for players to only wear numbers between one and 22.

“It’s amazing, my emotions are high right now, just trying to take it all in... I’m so happy,” Torpey said.

"It's just so nice to be back with the girls, back playing for my country and to come on and score a goal, it's a dream come true" ðŸ¥¹ðŸŽ¤ Hear from Michelle Heyman post-match following our 3-0 win over Uzbekistan.ðŸŽ¥:@10FootballAU#Matildas#TilitsDone#AllezAUS#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/OiHbZ49B8L

Matildas coach Tony Gustavsson switched up his attack strategy with Torpey coming off and Caitlin Foord coming on, which proved effective.

Mary Fowler doubled the Matildas’ lead in the 84th minute with Foord banging the nail in the coffin two minutes later.

CF9 ðŸ¤ SC7#Matildas#TilitsDone#AllezAUS#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/DqNrWu4beq

The Matildas will face Uzbekistan at the Marvel Stadium in Melbourne on Wednesday at 8pm with an aggregate score of 3-0.

Despite the freezing conditions in Tashkent, it is expected to reach 36C in the Victorian capital this week with more goals expected for the Matildas who have all but secured their tickets to Paris later in the year.

As only 18 players are able to be selected for the Olympics, instead of 23 as in the World Cup, the Tillies’ coach will have a lot to consider when it comes to his team’s structure after Wednesday.
